page: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration provides 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. A key characteristic of this specific instance type is its high memory density, offering 504.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 31.5 GB of memory per core. This constrained-vCPU configuration is designed to support memory-intensive workloads while qualitatively optimizing software licensing costs tied to vCPU counts.

In terms of performance, the server demonstrates solid capabilities across standard benchmarks. The Intel Xeon processor delivers average single-core and multi-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1573.9 and a multi-core score of 12871.56. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 695.21 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and maintaining 99.79 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, certain single-threaded tasks show limitations; for instance, the single-threaded bzip3 decompression speed is categorized as weak at 8.55 GB/sec, whereas multi-threaded decompression performs at an average level of 66.16 GB/sec.

The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage, which the ESv4 family supports. This makes the server highly suitable for memory-heavy, non-graphical workloads. It is particularly well-suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, where it achieves an average of 13.12 million SET operations per second, and enterprise applications that require massive memory footprints but do not demand high parallel CPU scaling.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 2.8 GHz clock speed. The server stands out for its massive memory capacity of 504.0 GB, providing 31.5 GB of RAM per core, which offers high resource density for memory-bound applications while optimizing software licensing costs. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show average CPU and memory performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12871.56 and a Redis throughput of 13.12 million SET operations per second. This configuration is highly suited for large database deployments, in-memory analytics, and caching layers.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 504 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive database workloads and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support for database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 504 GB of RAM and 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs for memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
504.0 GB of RAM providing an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 31.5 GB per core
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or GPUs bundled
Average multi-core performance with 12,871.56 stress-ng score and 13.12 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications
